rulings.Case Title: GENE ESCALERA et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, May 25, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant The Pep Boys, Manny, Moe & Jack of California¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar. The defendant was dismissed on April 28, 2010. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WALLACE CAWELTI et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDENTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, May 25, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Canoga Chrysler-Plymouth, Inc.¿s motion for withdrawal of attorney of record is granted. No opposition was filed. Hearing required unless a representative of Canoga Chrysler-Plymouth advises a representative of the Lerner Veit firm that Canoga will not contest the tentative ruling. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD ALLEN COTTRELL VS. AUTOZONE, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, May 25, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Hewlett-Packard Company¿s motion to strike the portions of plaintiff¿s complaint regarding plaintiff¿s claim for punitive damages is granted. No opposition was filed.

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD ALLEN COTTRELL VS. AUTOZONE, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, May 25, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Hewlett-Packard Company¿s demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff failed to identify the product to which plaintiff¿s father was exposed as required by Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical Co., Inc. (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71, 80. As guidance for the parties going forward, the court finds the allegations that Hewlett-Packard engaged in fraudulent conduct are alleged with requisite specificity. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CALVIN OXFORD et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION For Reconsideration Of Order Determining Issues To Be Retried, Based Upon New And Different Facts [Ccp § 1008 (B)]
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, May 25, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Plaintiff¿s motion for reconsideration of order determining issues to be retried is denied. The court of appeal¿s statement in its denial of plaintiff¿s petition for a writ of mandate/prohibition that ¿retrial is limited to issues involving failure to warn¿ includes the government contractor defense to plaintiff¿s failure to warn claims. Oxford v. Foster Wheeler (2009)177 Cal.App.4th 700 did not decide that there was insufficient evidence to support a government contractor defense as to failure to warn claims. The court found that ¿the jury was not given a government contractor defense instruction tailored to the failure to warn context.¿ (Id. at 712). =(220/HEK)
